content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer NNN, find the nearest multiple of 333.

Multiples of 333 are ‚Ä¶‚ąí6,‚ąí3,0,3,6,9,12,15,‚Ä¶\ldots -6, -3, 0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, \ldots‚Ä¶‚ąí6,‚ąí3,0,3,6,9,12,15,‚Ä¶.

Formally, find XXX, the multiple of 333 with minimum value of ‚ą£N‚ąíX‚ą£|N - X|‚ą£N‚ąíX‚ą£.

It can be proven that the answer is always unique, i.e. 222 different multiples of 333 cannot be nearest at the same time.


Input Format

The only line of input contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the nearest multiple of 333.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The nearest multiple is 000.
For example, 333 is not the answer because ‚ą£1‚ąí0‚ą£=1<‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£=2|1 - 0| = 1 < |1 - 3| = 2‚ą£1‚ąí0‚ą£=1<‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£=2.

Explanation:
The nearest multiple of 333 is 666.

Explanation:
999 is itself a multiple of 333.
